commit-msg-generator.notes: Both produce correct Conventional Commits for clear descriptions. with_skill adds decision rationale and better convention matching.

state-scanner.notes: Key differentiator: with_skill produces interactive numbered workflow selection [1]-[4] with confidence scoring. without_skill gives prioritized advice (P0/P1/P2) but no interactive selection format.

spec-drafter.notes: Both produce comprehensive Level 3 specs. with_skill generates separate proposal.md + tasks.md (correct Level 3 convention) with level judgment visualization. without_skill produces single combined doc with excellent content (scope boundary, detailed risks).

evals.failure_detail: without_skill did not present interactive numbered options for workflow selection — gave prioritized advice instead

cross_project_findings: state-scanner shows the clearest cross-project value (+0.25): its structured interactive workflow (numbered options + confidence scores) is a unique capability vanilla Claude doesn't replicate
commit-msg-generator and spec-drafter show EQUAL verdicts on Kairos: vanilla Claude handles clear descriptions and spec writing well without guidance

Skills with strong PROCESS value (state-scanner's interactive selection, aria-report's privacy review) transfer better cross-project than CONTENT skills (commit-msg, spec-drafter) where Claude's base capability is already strong
Token usage: with_skill uses more tokens for state-scanner (45K vs 21K) but delivers richer output (confidence scoring, multi-phase diagnostics, architecture assessment)

portability_ranking.pattern: Process-oriented Skills (workflow selection, safety checks) > Content-oriented Skills (message generation, doc writing)
